claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects target-project file mutations and blocks PRD readiness on violations, with comprehensive evidence scope validation.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA256 hash snapshot of all project files excluding allowed write roots
evidence.why: Implements core boundary detection by fingerprinting project state before agent execution

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: Detects any project mutations during evidence agent execution

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime creates boundary snapshots before and after all agent executions, then diffs to detect changes
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation that surrounds full-tool agent execution with file system monitoring

evidence.summary: Boundary changes are detected and result.boundary.project_mutation is set to 'violated' if any files changed outside allowed roots
evidence.why: Implements violation detection that triggers PRD readiness blockers

evidence.summary: Boundary violations create BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force readiness.prd_ready to false
evidence.why: Connects boundary audit results to PRD readiness blocking logic

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks that required evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) have completed with valid results
evidence.why: Ensures evidence gathering is complete before PRD readiness can pass

evidence.summary: For complete evidence results, checks for scope declaration issues and project evidence path requirements
evidence.why: Validates evidence quality and prevents assumptions from being treated as facts

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope validates that evidence records declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Enforces scope declaration requirement for evidence validation

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues creates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers when evidence records lack proper scope declarations
evidence.why: Blocks PRD readiness when evidence scope is not properly declared

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues creates blockers when project-scoped evidence lacks repo-relative paths or when existing-project facts lack project evidence
evidence.why: Ensures project facts are backed by concrete project evidence with file locators

evidence.summary: execution_policy in dispatch plan defines boundary.project_mutation as 'forbidden' and lists allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Documents the boundary contract that evidence agents must follow

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly states 'Boundary: do not modify target project files'
evidence.why: Communicates boundary constraint directly to evidence agents

risks: If boundary snapshot mechanism fails silently or has filesystem permission issues, mutations could go undetected
If agents modify files during execution but revert changes before snapshot, violations would not be detected
If allowed_write_roots configuration is misaligned with actual artifact directories, false positives could occur

result.notes: Boundary audit is implemented with SHA256 fingerprinting, pre/post execution comparison, and automatic blocker generation. Evidence scope validation is comprehensive with multiple checkpoint layers.
